rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE content, with type scopes for constants and equations, and implementation scopes for assignments.
generic_rules: Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions: numeric variables must use numeric expressions, and Boolean variables must use Boolean expressions.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the model's configured dependencies.
Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex scope to avoid unresolved references.
Write stateful equations with initialized temporal recurrence and branching logic with nested if-then-else structures.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts use assume/guarantee syntax with labeled expressions and eq/const declarations.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes are for assignments.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing parameters such as Compute_Execution_Time, Deadline, Period, Priority, etc.
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair actions
Model unit and package qualification in AGREE expressions
